existing_reusable_interfaces.reuse: Keep unchanged as the per-candidate pure compiler.
Instantiate inside one checkpoint coordinator; do not make recorder callers manage it.
Consume only a completed diagnostic outcome projected to pending_counterexample_comparisons; do not add candidate evaluation or cache logic to the controller.
Use unchanged behind an evidence Adapter after a complete symmetric checkpoint.
Continue consuming CorpusItem only after scoped behavior memory has passed its own retrieval gate.
Reuse character budgeting and injection audit; do not teach it order-regime semantics.

gaps.evidence: FMEPilotEvidenceRecorder.record_candidate is called one candidate at a time and immediately mutates archives and local score maps.
gaps.risk: It cannot prove that all 2-4 frozen candidates were evaluated under one profile, budget reservation, and implementation hash set.

gaps.evidence: Current manifest-to-runner configuration is a set of flat feedback-policy and training-profile strings; RunSpec carries no optional diagnostic policy.
gaps.risk: Adding another feedback-policy string would couple activation, profile choice, budget, and evidence projection across batch_runner and eoh_single_runner.
gaps.resolution_seam: OrderRegimeDiagnosticPolicy parsed as one immutable record with enabled=false default

gaps.evidence: CorpusItem contains generic text fields but no actor, evidence_level, applicability_scope, evidence hash, or content hash.
gaps.risk: Writing the diagnostic directly as a CorpusItem would make scope enforcement depend on prose and allow development evidence to be retrieved as general quality knowledge.
gaps.resolution_seam: ScopedBehaviorMemory plus a gated CorpusItem Adapter

gaps.evidence: FMEControllerState has pending_counterexample_comparisons but no candidate set, trigger reason, cache key, or reserved diagnostic budget.
gaps.risk: Putting those fields into FMEControllerState would widen the controller Interface with problem-specific detail.
gaps.resolution_seam: Project only completed outcome counts into existing state; keep trigger data inside the coordinator.

gaps.evidence: PromptContext already renders generic CorpusItem records and audits character use.
gaps.risk: Adding order-regime branches would create a shallow pass-through and spread semantics into presentation code.
gaps.resolution_seam: Build a bounded memory summary before converting to CorpusItem.

minimal_future_modules.interface: diagnose(checkpoint: FrozenCandidateCheckpoint, policy: OrderRegimeDiagnosticPolicy, evaluator: OrderRegimeEvaluator) -> OrderRegimeDiagnosticOutcome
minimal_future_modules.depth: Hide activation, 2-4 candidate validation, near-tie reason validation, symmetric budget reservation, exact cache keys, evaluation ordering, failure retention, summary compilation, ranking tracking, and outcome hashing.
minimal_future_modules.adapter_rule: The existing BP development evaluator is the production Adapter; a deterministic in-memory evaluator is the future verification Adapter, making this a real seam.
minimal_future_modules.default_behavior: OrderRegimeDiagnosticPolicy.enabled=false returns a skipped outcome without constructing the evaluator.

minimal_future_modules.depth: Hide archive artifacts, DecisionRecord inputs, typed scoped memories, deduplication hashes, bounded prompt summaries, and provenance fields behind one pure projection.
minimal_future_modules.adapter_rule: Persistence adapters consume the projection; the Module itself performs no file I/O.

minimal_existing_file_touches_for_a_futu.change: Export the two new Module interfaces only.
Add immutable checkpoint, outcome, and scoped-memory records only if keeping them in the shared contract package reduces duplicate serialization.

deferred_until_a_separately_frozen_runna.reason: No runnable manifest is authorized; parsing activation now would create a launch surface.
Do not add CLI forwarding or another evolution_feedback_policy value.
Do not wire profile construction or candidate evaluation before a separate launch contract.
Do not add checkpoint orchestration to the per-candidate recorder; a future persistence Adapter may consume the pure projection.
Do not load scoped memories through generic extra_corpus_paths until the applicability gate is implemented.
Do not widen every CorpusItem caller with order-specific evidence fields; use a typed domain record and gated Adapter.

couplings_to_avoid: Do not encode activation as a new evolution_feedback_policy string.
Do not let batch_runner calculate near ties, candidate-set hashes, or diagnostic budgets.
Do not let FMEController evaluate candidates, construct BP profiles, or own cache entries.
Do not call OrderRegimeRankingTracker from multiple recorder calls without an explicit checkpoint owner.
Do not add order-regime branches to PromptContext or the generic keyword retriever.
Do not serialize scoped behavior memory directly as a generic CorpusItem before applicability and evidence gates pass.
Do not reuse the FME v2 manifest, profile, recorder state, result directories, or held-out coordinates.

deletion_test.coordinator: Deleting it would redistribute trigger, budget, cache, symmetry, failure, and ranking logic across at least runner, controller, and recorder, so the Module earns its seam.
deletion_test.projector: Deleting it would duplicate provenance, scope, hashing, archive, prompt, and memory projection rules across persistence and RAG callers, so the Module earns its seam.

evidence_refs.purpose: existing generic RAG Interface and extra-corpus path
